title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in Irving, Texas
twitter: 🚛 Semi crash in Irving, TX? The truck's black box data could be the key to your case — but it gets overwritten FAST. Don't wait. Call 855-WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) now. https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/black-box-data-after-a-semi-crash-in-irving-texas
linkedin:
If you or someone you represent has been involved in a semi-truck collision in Irving, Texas, one of the most critical — and time-sensitive — pieces of evidence is the commercial vehicle's black box, also known as an Event Data Recorder (EDR) or Electronic Logging Device (ELD).

This data can capture vehicle speed, braking patterns, hours of service, and driver behavior in the seconds before impact. But here's the problem: trucking companies are not required to preserve this data indefinitely, and it can be overwritten within days.

For attorneys in our network of 800+ law firms across Texas and nationwide, WreckMatch connects seriously injured victims with the right legal representation — fast. When black box data is at stake, timing is everything. A preservation letter or spoliation notice needs to go out immediately.

If you have a client involved in a semi crash in Irving or anywhere along the I-635 or SH-114 corridors, now is the time to act. WreckMatch helps match victims with experienced truck accident attorneys who know how to secure this evidence before it disappears.

facebook:
If you've been in a semi-truck crash in Irving, Texas, the last thing on your mind is data preservation — but it may be the most important step you can take right now. 🚛

Commercial trucks are equipped with black boxes that record speed, braking, and driver behavior right before a crash. That information could be the difference between a strong case and a lost one. The problem? That data doesn't last forever. Trucking companies can allow it to be overwritten quickly, sometimes within days.

We know how overwhelming it is after a serious crash. Between injuries, insurance calls, and just trying to get back on your feet, the legal details feel impossible. That's why having the right attorney — one who moves fast and knows trucking law — matters so much.

WreckMatch helps injured people in Irving and across Texas find experienced attorneys who handle semi-truck cases and know exactly how to protect critical evidence.

Have you or someone you love ever dealt with the aftermath of a truck accident and felt like important evidence was already gone?
reddit_title: Semi truck hit me in Irving, TX — how important is the black box data and how do I make sure it's preserved?
reddit_body:
If you were recently in a crash involving a commercial semi-truck in Irving or anywhere in the DFW area, one of the first things you should know is that most big rigs are equipped with an Event Data Recorder (EDR) and Electronic Logging Device (ELD). These record speed, braking, hours driven, and other critical data from before the crash. That information can be incredibly valuable in establishing fault.

The catch is that this data isn't preserved automatically. Trucking companies are not legally required to hold onto it indefinitely, and it can be overwritten in as little as 30 days — sometimes sooner. The moment you suspect the trucking company may be at fault, a formal legal preservation letter (or spoliation notice) should go out. This is something only an attorney can do effectively, and timing is everything.

If you're not sure where to start, sites like wreckmatch.com help connect truck accident victims with attorneys who specifically handle commercial vehicle cases and know how to act fast on evidence like black box data. You shouldn't have to figure this out alone, especially when the clock is already ticking.
